executiveSummary: The ADE - Trade semantic model has 45 tables (9 DirectQuery, 29 Dual, 7 Import) with 801 measures across a 6-billion-row daily trade snapshot fact table. The model spans 5 Databricks catalogs (sales, product, technology, supplychain, sourcingandbuying) with all serve-layer models materialised as views. Critical performance issues stem from: (1) a massive DirectQuery fact table (6.05B rows) as the central hub with 21 relationships, (2) 49 critical-complexity DAX measures generating up to 14 estimated SQL subqueries each, (3) 228 measures using ALL/ALLEXCEPT filter clearing patterns that force full re-aggregation, (4) 11 Dual-mode tables that could safely switch to Import, (5) 650 unused column candidates inflating scan width, and (6) 12 report pages averaging 22+ visuals each without query reduction settings enabled. The combination of wide column selection, cross-catalog JOINs, and no date filtering on serve views creates severe scan amplification on every user interaction.

rootCauseFindings.title: 6-Billion-Row Fact Table as DirectQuery Hub

impact.latency: Every visual touching Daily Trade generates SQL scanning billions of rows. With degree 21 (most connected table), nearly all report pages trigger queries against this table.
impact.cost: Each query reads the full fact table width (97 columns) via pass-through serve view, multiplied by the number of visuals per page.

evidence: Daily Trade: 6,052,560,794 rows, directQuery mode, degree 21 (hub table)
97 columns in fact table, 14 identified as removal candidates
94 measure references to Daily Trade
Serve view fact_product_option_trade_daily_snapshot_v1 has no WHERE filter

recommendations.action: Add date-based WHERE filter to serve_fact_product_option_trade_daily_snapshot_v1
recommendations.detail: Filter to rolling 2-year window (matching PBI date slicer range) to reduce scan from 6B to ~2.2B rows (~960 days × 6.3M rows/day)

recommendations.action: Create a pre-aggregated materialised view for common trade metrics
recommendations.detail: Aggregate daily trade by date + product option + territory to reduce row count by 10-100x for most visuals

recommendations.action: Remove 14 unreferenced columns from the Daily Trade table in the semantic model
recommendations.detail: Reduces bytes scanned per query. Physical Retail Return columns (9 columns) and other unreferenced columns add ~166 GB estimated scan overhead

rootCauseFindings.title: DAX Pattern Families Amplify Query Cost

impact.latency: 204 Last Year comparison measures and 116 Last Week comparison measures each use SUMX + FILTER(ALL) + time intelligence, generating 10-14 SQL subqueries per measure evaluation.
impact.cost: Pattern families share identical anti-pattern structures — fixing the template once fixes hundreds of measures.

evidence: 49 critical-complexity measures (score 44-45), each with 5-7 context transitions and 5-6 FILTER(ALL) calls
204 measures in 'Last Year Comparison' family (critical severity)
116 measures in 'Last Week Comparison' family (critical severity)
55 measures in 'Average Weekly Cover' family (critical severity)
77 total measures using FILTER(ALL) anti-pattern
Top measure generates 14 estimated SQL subqueries

recommendations.action: Refactor Last Year/Last Week measure templates to use CALCULATE with DATEADD instead of SUMX + FILTER(ALL)
recommendations.detail: Replace SUMX(FILTER(ALL(Date), ...), CALCULATE(...)) with CALCULATE([BaseMeasure], DATEADD(Date[calendar_date], -1, YEAR)). Eliminates row-by-row iteration and full table filter clearing. Fixes 320+ measures via template change.

recommendations.action: Refactor Average Weekly Cover measures to pre-compute weekly aggregates
recommendations.detail: 55 measures use iterator patterns over weekly windows. Pre-computing weekly aggregates in a calculated table or materialised view eliminates the SUMX cost.

recommendations.action: Extract repeated CALCULATE subexpressions into VAR statements
recommendations.detail: 108 measures have repeated subexpressions. Using VAR guarantees single evaluation, potentially halving subquery count.

rootCauseFindings.title: 29 Dual-Mode Tables Cause Double Processing

impact.latency: Dual tables are refreshed in Import mode AND queried via DirectQuery, doubling processing during refresh cycles.
impact.cost: 11 Dual tables have no direct relationship to DirectQuery tables and can safely switch to Import without functionality loss.

evidence: 29 tables in Dual mode out of 45 total
11 tables safe to switch to Import: Daily Financial Half/Month/Year to Date, Daily/Weekly Financial Transform, Earliest Global dates, Time Period, Weekly Financial references
Daily Financial Half to Date (2.2M rows) and Daily Financial Year to Date (2.96M rows) are the largest unnecessarily Dual tables
BPA rule DUAL_MODE_TABLES flagged 25 violations

recommendations.action: Switch 11 safe Dual tables to Import mode
recommendations.detail: Tables with no direct DQ relationship: Daily Financial Half/Month/Year to Date, Daily/Weekly Financial Transform, Earliest Global Available To Sell Date, Earliest Global Go Live Date, Time Period, Weekly Financial Half/Month/Year to Date. Eliminates unnecessary DQ queries for these tables.

recommendations.action: Evaluate remaining 18 Dual tables for Import conversion
recommendations.detail: Small dimension tables (Price Status: 6 rows, Pricing Store: 19 rows, etc.) could be Import if RELATED() usage is verified. Requires measure-by-measure audit.

rootCauseFindings.title: Cross-Catalog DirectQuery JOINs

impact.latency: PBI generates SQL that JOINs tables from 5 different Databricks catalogs (sales, product, technology, supplychain, sourcingandbuying) in a single query. Cross-catalog JOINs prevent query optimisation and increase network overhead.
impact.cost: Each catalog boundary adds serialisation overhead. The SQL Warehouse must coordinate across multiple storage locations.

evidence: Source groups span 5 catalogs: dq:sales.serve (10 tables), dq:technology.serve (15 tables), dq:product.serve (9 tables), dq:supplychain.serve (3 tables), dq:sourcingandbuying.serve (1 table)
Daily Trade hub (sales.serve) has relationships to tables in all 5 catalogs
All serve models are pass-through views (SELECT * FROM curated_table)

recommendations.action: Convert small cross-catalog dimension tables to Import mode
recommendations.detail: Country (255 rows), Territory (5 rows), Warehouse (20 rows), Supplier (6,474 rows) from non-sales catalogs are small enough for Import. Eliminates cross-catalog JOINs for these tables.

recommendations.action: Add column projection to serve views
recommendations.detail: Replace SELECT * with explicit column lists in serve views. Currently all 97 fact columns are exposed when PBI only references ~83 in measures.

rootCauseFindings.title: Report Visuals Overloaded Without Query Reduction

impact.latency: 12 pages with 274 total visuals. Pages like 'Topline Performance Date' (31 visuals) and 'Topline Performance' (28 visuals) fire 20-30+ simultaneous DirectQuery requests on page load. Without query reduction settings, every slicer change and cross-highlight fires additional queries.
impact.cost: 19 wide matrix visuals project 10-43 columns each, amplifying bytes read per visual.

evidence: 12/12 pages exceed 15-visual threshold (V01: 12 findings)
19 wide matrix visuals (V06), worst case: 43 projected columns
35 report-level filters (V03, threshold: 10)
Query reduction: cross-highlighting enabled, slicer/filter Apply buttons disabled
12 large embedded images (V08)

recommendations.action: Enable query reduction settings: disable cross-highlighting, enable Apply buttons on slicers and filters
recommendations.detail: In PBI Desktop → File → Options → Report settings → Query reduction. This alone can reduce DQ query volume by 50-70% during user interaction.

recommendations.action: Split heavy pages using bookmarks or drillthrough
recommendations.detail: Topline Performance (28 visuals) and Daily Trade Overview (22 visuals) should be split. Use bookmarks to show/hide visual groups, or drillthrough pages for detail views.

recommendations.action: Reduce matrix column counts
recommendations.detail: 19 matrices exceed 10 columns. The 43-column matrix on Topline Performance is a major query amplifier. Use conditional columns or drillthrough for rarely-used fields.

rootCauseFindings.title: 650 Unused Column Candidates Inflate Scan Width

impact.cost: BPA identified 650 columns not referenced by any measure, relationship, or hierarchy. In DirectQuery mode, PBI may still request these columns in generated SQL, increasing bytes read.
impact.memory: For Dual/Import tables, unused columns consume VertiPaq memory during refresh.

evidence: 650 UNUSED_COLUMNS_CANDIDATE findings in BPA
UDA table: 134 columns, 118 removal candidates
Option table: 63 columns, 46 removal candidates
Daily Trade: 97 columns, 14 removal candidates (estimated 166 GB scan savings)

recommendations.action: Hide or remove unreferenced columns from the semantic model
recommendations.detail: Start with Daily Trade (14 columns, highest impact due to 6B rows), then UDA (118 columns), then Option (46 columns). Mark columns as hidden first to test for user-facing dependencies.

rootCauseFindings.title: 3 Bidirectional Relationships Create Ambiguous Filter Paths

impact.latency: Bidirectional cross-filtering on Option↔Option Ranking, Time Period↔Date, and Product Option Fabric Composition↔Option creates multiple filter propagation paths. The engine must evaluate all paths, increasing query complexity.

evidence: 3 bidirectional relationships detected
Option.Dim Product Option Sk ↔ Option Ranking.dim_product_option_sk
Time Period.dim_date_sk ↔ Date.Dim Date Sk
Product Option Fabric Composition.Dim Product Option Sk ↔ Option.Dim Product Option Sk

recommendations.action: Change bidirectional relationships to one-direction where possible
recommendations.detail: Review each relationship to determine if bidirectional filtering is required. Use CROSSFILTER() in DAX for specific measures that need reverse filtering instead of model-level bidirectional.

rootCauseFindings.title: dbt Serve Views Missing Clustering and Date Filters

impact.latency: 6 curated tables backing Trade-consumed serve views lack liquid clustering. 2 serve views over large fact tables have no WHERE filter, exposing the full table history to every PBI query.

evidence: E02: 6 curated tables missing liquid clustering (product.curated dimensions with 2.5M-44.6M rows)
E04: 2 serve views over large tables without WHERE filters
E05: 6 models with functions on filter columns preventing predicate pushdown
E07: 6 models using ROW_NUMBER subquery instead of QUALIFY
E15: 2 serve views recommended for materialised_view conversion

recommendations.action: Add liquid clustering to 6 curated product dimension tables
recommendations.detail: dim_product_option_country_restriction, dim_product_option_fabric_composition, dim_product_option_multi_valued_uda, dim_product_option_single_valued_uda, dim_product_option_in_store, dim_product_option_in_warehouse. Cluster by dim_product_option_sk for JOIN predicate pushdown.

recommendations.action: Add date WHERE filter to fact serve view
recommendations.detail: serve_fact_product_option_trade_daily_snapshot_v1 should filter to rolling 2-3 year window. Reduces scan from 6B rows (960 days since 2023-09-04) to match actual PBI date range.

recommendations.action: Convert 2 high-traffic serve views to materialised views
recommendations.detail: E15 flagged 2 serve views that would benefit from materialisation. Pre-computed results eliminate view-expansion overhead.

rootCauseFindings.title: 220 Snowflake DirectQuery Chains Multiply Subqueries

impact.latency: BPA identified 220 snowflake DQ chains — sequences of DirectQuery tables connected through relationships. Each chain forces nested subqueries in generated SQL, with depth up to 4 levels.

evidence: 220 SNOWFLAKE_DQ_CHAINS findings
Max snowflake depth: 4
Hub table Daily Trade connects to chains through Option (bridge, degree 9) to Product Option sub-tables
Each relationship hop adds a nested subquery with full column projection

recommendations.action: Flatten the deepest DQ chains by denormalising into the serve view
recommendations.detail: The Daily Trade → Option → Product Option In Store/Warehouse/Pricing Store chains (depth 3-4) should be pre-joined in the serve layer. This eliminates 2-3 levels of nested subqueries.

recommendations.action: Convert leaf-node dimension tables in chains to Import
recommendations.detail: Tables at the end of DQ chains (Stock Age Band: 1,043 rows, Inventory Status: 22 rows) can be Import without significant freshness impact, breaking the DQ chain.

rootCauseFindings.title: 115 Measures Exposed to MDX Without Need

impact.cost: 115 measures have isAvailableInMdx enabled but may not need Excel/SSRS connectivity. Each MDX-visible measure increases metadata overhead.

evidence: 115 IS_AVAILABLE_IN_MDX findings in BPA

recommendations.action: Disable isAvailableInMdx for internal/helper measures
recommendations.detail: Review measures and disable MDX availability for those not consumed by Excel or SSRS connections.

rootCauseFindings.title: 3 Calculated Tables and 11 Unmarked Date Tables

impact.latency: 3 calculated tables (ALB Bracket, column_selector, OptionConditionSelector) are computed at refresh time. 11 date-related tables not marked as date tables prevents auto time intelligence optimisation.
impact.quality: Unmarked date tables may cause incorrect time intelligence behaviour.

evidence: 3 CALCULATED_TABLES findings
11 DATE_TABLE_NOT_MARKED findings
3 TIME_INTEL_ON_DQ findings — time intelligence functions used on DirectQuery tables

recommendations.action: Mark primary date table as Date Table in the model
recommendations.detail: Mark Date (dim_date_v2) as the model's date table. This enables auto date/time optimisations and prevents PBI from creating hidden auto-date tables.

actionMatrix.action: Enable query reduction settings in Trade report
actionMatrix.estimatedImprovement: 50-70% reduction in DQ query volume during interaction
Reduce fact scan from 6B to ~2.2B rows per query

actionMatrix.action: Switch 11 safe Dual tables to Import mode
actionMatrix.location: Daily Financial HTD/MTD/YTD, Weekly Financial refs, Time Period, Earliest Global dates
actionMatrix.estimatedImprovement: Eliminates DQ queries for 11 tables, reduces refresh double-processing

actionMatrix.action: Refactor Last Year/Last Week DAX templates
actionMatrix.estimatedImprovement: Reduce from 14 SQL subqueries to 2-3 per measure evaluation

actionMatrix.location: Option↔Option Ranking, Time Period↔Date, Fabric Composition↔Option
actionMatrix.estimatedImprovement: Removes ambiguous filter paths, simplifies query plans

actionMatrix.action: Add liquid clustering to 6 product curated tables
actionMatrix.estimatedImprovement: Enables data skipping on JOIN predicates for 2.5M-44.6M row tables

actionMatrix.action: Remove 14 unreferenced columns from Daily Trade
actionMatrix.estimatedImprovement: ~14% reduction in fact table scan width

actionMatrix.location: Topline Performance, Topline Performance Date pages
actionMatrix.estimatedImprovement: Reduce simultaneous DQ queries from 28-31 to 12-15 per page load

actionMatrix.action: Create pre-aggregated materialised view for daily trade
Flatten deep DQ chains by denormalising Option sub-tables
actionMatrix.estimatedImprovement: Eliminates 2-3 levels of nested subqueries per visual

actionMatrix.action: Convert small cross-catalog dimensions to Import
actionMatrix.estimatedImprovement: Eliminates cross-catalog JOINs for small lookup tables
Reduce bytes read per visual by limiting column projection

skippedSteps.name: Databricks Metadata Profiling
skippedSteps.reason: Databricks MCP permission error on Technology SQL Warehouse. Volumetry from CSV used instead.

skippedSteps.name: Query Profiling
skippedSteps.reason: No Performance Analyzer export provided and Databricks query history unavailable due to permission error.
